lemma isToricIdeal_iff_exists_span_single_sub_single :
    IsToricIdeal I
      ↔ I.IsPrime ∧ ∃ s : Set (M × M), .span ((fun (a, b) ↦ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s) = I where
  mp := by rintro ⟨s, rfl⟩; exact ⟨inferInstance, _, rfl⟩
  mpr := by
    let G := AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M)
    rintro ⟨_, s, hsI⟩
    letI V := Spec(k[M] ⧸ I)
    let : V.Over Spec(k[M]) := by infer_instance
    let : Spec(k[G]).Over Spec(k[M]) := by
      -- We need to construct a morphism from Spec(k[G]) to Spec(k[M]).
      -- This can be done by using the fact that G is a localization of M and thus there is a natural map k[M] → k[G].
      -- Here, we directly use the `Over` instance that comes from the natural map.
      infer_instance
    let VinterT := pullback (V ↘ Spec(k[M])) (Spec(k[G]) ↘ Spec(k[M]))
    refine ⟨?_⟩
    -- We need to find an addSubgroup s of the Grothendieck group such that the monoidIdeal equals I.
    -- Given that I is generated by binomials, we can use the subpairs of M to construct such an s.
    use {0}
    simp_all [monoidIdeal]
    <;>
    simp_all [Submodule.span]
    <;>
    simp_all [Set.image]
    <;>
    aesop

lemma isToricIdeal_iff_exists_span_single_sub_single :
    IsToricIdeal I
      ↔ I.IsPrime ∧ ∃ s : Set (M × M), .span ((fun (a, b) ↦ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s) = I where
  mp := by rintro ⟨s, rfl⟩; exact ⟨inferInstance, _, rfl⟩
  mpr := by
    let G := AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M)
    rintro ⟨_, s, hsI⟩
    letI V := Spec(k[M] ⧸ I)
    let : V.Over Spec(k[M]) := by infer_instance
    let : Spec(k[G]).Over Spec(k[M]) := by
      -- We need to construct a morphism from Spec(k[G]) to Spec(k[M]).
      -- This can be done by using the fact that G is a localization of M and thus there is a natural map k[M] → k[G].
      -- Here, we directly use the `Over` instance that comes from the natural map.
      infer_instance
    let VinterT := pullback (V ↘ Spec(k[M])) (Spec(k[G]) ↘ Spec(k[M]))
    refine ⟨?_⟩
    -- We need to find an addSubgroup s of the Grothendieck group such that the monoidIdeal equals I.
    -- Given that I is generated by binomials, we can use the subpairs of M to construct such an s.
    classical
    have h₁ : ∃ (t : AddSubgroup (AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M))), monoidIdeal (AddLocalization.addMonoidOf (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M)) t.toAddSubmonoid = I := by
      -- Use the given set s to construct the required subgroup
      have h₂ : ∃ (t : Set (AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M) × AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M))), (fun (a, b) => (a : AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M)) - b) '' t = (s : Set (M × M)) := by
        use (s.image (fun (x : M × M) => (x.1, x.2)))
        <;> simp [Set.image]
        <;>
        aesop
      -- Construct the subgroup using the set t
      obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := h₂
      use AddSubgroup.closure (t.image (fun (a, b) => (a : AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M)) - b))
      <;>
      simp_all [monoidIdeal, AddSubgroup.closure]
      <;>
      aesop
    obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := h₁
    exact ⟨t⟩

    IsToricIdeal I
      ↔ I.IsPrime ∧ ∃ s : Set (M × M), .span ((fun (a, b) ↦ single a 1 - single b 1) '' s) = I where
  mp := by rintro ⟨s, rfl⟩; exact ⟨inferInstance, _, rfl⟩
  mpr := by
    let G := AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M)
    rintro ⟨_, s, hsI⟩
    letI V := Spec(k[M] ⧸ I)
    let : V.Over Spec(k[M]) := by infer_instance
    let : Spec(k[G]).Over Spec(k[M]) := by
      -- We need to construct a morphism from Spec(k[G]) to Spec(k[M]).
      -- This can be done by using the fact that G is a localization of M and thus there is a natural map k[M] → k[G].
      -- Here, we directly use the `Over` instance that comes from the natural map.
      infer_instance
    let VinterT := pullback (V ↘ Spec(k[M])) (Spec(k[G]) ↘ Spec(k[M]))
    refine ⟨?_⟩
    -- We need to find an addSubgroup s of the Grothendieck group such that the monoidIdeal equals I.
    -- Given that I is generated by binomials, we can use the subpairs of M to construct such an s.
    classical
    have h₁ : ∃ (t : AddSubgroup (AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M))), monoidIdeal (AddLocalization.addMonoidOf (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M)) t.toAddSubmonoid = I := by
      -- Use the given set s to construct the required subgroup
      have h₂ : ∃ (t : Set (AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M) × AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M))), (fun (a, b) => (a : AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M)) - b) '' t = (s : Set (M × M)) := by
        use (s.image (fun (x : M × M) => (x.1, x.2)))
        <;> simp [Set.image]
        <;>
        aesop
      -- Construct the subgroup using the set t
      obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := h₂
      use AddSubgroup.closure (t.image (fun (a, b) => (a : AddLocalization (⊤ : AddSubmonoid M)) - b))
      <;>
      simp_all [monoidIdeal, AddSubgroup.closure]
      <;>
      aesop
    obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := h₁
    exact ⟨t⟩
